result.summary: Mapped 1 HTTP API surface (Apigee control-plane REST API at apigee.googleapis.com) and 1 CLI surface (apigeecli); both authenticate with Google Cloud-issued OAuth access tokens, and the CLI can also derive them from a service account JSON key.

gcp_oauth_access_token.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token for Apigee
gcp_oauth_access_token.setup: Easiest path: install and sign in with the Google Cloud CLI, then mint an access token with `gcloud auth print-access-token`. For local ADC-backed use, you can also run [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login). The `apigeecli` README shows using `gcloud auth print-access-token` directly against Apigee APIs. You must have access to an Apigee-enabled Google Cloud project/org.

gcp_service_account_key.label: Google Cloud service account JSON key
gcp_service_account_key.setup: Create or select a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts), grant it the needed Apigee/IAM permissions, then create and download a JSON key. The `apigeecli` docs show using this file with `apigeecli token gen -a serviceaccount.json` or by passing `-a`/`--account` so the CLI can obtain an access token for Apigee.

variables.name: organization
variables.resolveFrom: your Apigee organization / Google Cloud project
variables.description: Many Apigee API resource paths are under `v1/organizations/{organization}`.

packages.runtimeHint: Go-based standalone binary; container image also available at ghcr.io/apigee/apigeecli
